summary: High‑energy Hartford show with scorching Althea and seamless set‑2 flow
blurb: The first set bursts with driving jams on Sugaree, Peggy‑O and China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider, while the second set delivers a tight Althea, a fiery The Other One into Stella Blue, and a soaring Bertha. Jerry’s guitar is on fire throughout, Brent’s organ pushes the band forward, and the drums lock in with propulsive power.
review: On March 14 1981 at Veterans’ Memorial Coliseum in Hartford, the Dead turned in an electrifying performance that still shines for fans. The opening set never loses its momentum – “Sugaree” rolls out with a warm groove, “Peggy‑O” lands with crisp vocal harmonies, and the classic “China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider” segues flawlessly into a soaring jam. By the time they hit Set 2, the band is in full stride: “Althea” showcases Jerry’s blistering lead work and Brent’s organ crunch, while the “Lost Sailor > Saint Of Circumstance” transition glides into a thunderous drum duet that feeds straight into an incendiary “The Other One”. That momentum carries through to a heartfelt “Stella Blue”, a jubilant “Bertha”, and a rousing “Good Lovin’” to close. Across the board, reviewers note Jerry’s consistently dialed‑in tone – from Bach‑style teases to Green Sleeves flourishes – and Brent’s driving keyboard textures that define this early‑‘80s era. The drums, especially Billy Kreutzmann’s propulsive playing, keep the energy relentless, making this show a benchmark of the band’s high‑octane period.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Four out of six analyses are strongly positive, praising Jerry’s lead work, Brent’s organ, and the tight set‑2 flow; only one mixed review offers no criticism. Consistent language like 'blazing', 'scorching', 'excellent' across multiple recordings justifies a strong positive adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Excellent soundboard capture with crisp, clear audio and no dropouts; provides the most faithful representation of the band’s high‑energy performance.

key_highlights: First set delivers nonstop energy with standout jams on Sugaree and China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider
Althea shines with blistering guitar and driving organ, setting up a powerful second‑set momentum
The Lost Sailor → Saint Of Circumstance → The Other One sequence is seamless and incendiary
Brent’s organ work propels the band forward while Billy Kreutzmann’s drums lock in with tight, propulsive power

band_performance.Jerry: Consistently dialed‑in; blistering speed on Althea, Bertha and Sugaree, emotive delivery on Stella Blue, plus clever Bach and Green Sleeves teases.
band_performance.Brent: Driving organ work highlighted as a force that pushes the band forward; water‑splashy keyboard textures mark a breakthrough for the ’81 era.
band_performance.Billy: Tight, propulsive playing fuels The Other One and drives the high‑octane feel throughout the second set.
